name: Prototype Demo Bezel
description: Arcade-screen demo frame for product videos, interactive prototypes, image sequences, before-after states, or playable product previews.

demo-screen.description: Large bezel-framed product demo with controls, caption strip, status LEDs, and CTA.

slots.screen: Product video, screenshot, prototype, or interactive demo inside a thick bezel.
slots.control-strip: Play, pause, chapter, or mode controls styled as cabinet buttons.
slots.caption: Short explanation of what the viewer is seeing.
slots.status-leds: Tiny labeled state lights for Prototype, Shipping, Beta, or Live.
slots.demo-cta: Open full demo, watch walkthrough, or try prototype.

before-after-bezel.description: Single bezel with a before-after slider or mode switch to demonstrate product transformation.

slots.outcome-strip: Results or proof metrics tied to the demo.

visual_brief: A product demo that looks embedded in a physical arcade screen or product-testing console. The visual content should be real product evidence: screenshot, prototype, render, video frame, packaging, controller, or clear placeholder with aspect ratio. Avoid decorative abstract gradients as the demo. Controls should use recognizable icons and tactile rectangular arcade-button styling with hard press states. Mode controls should read as a clustered control group, with the primary reserve/watch action separated intentionally rather than scattered across the whole bezel.

responsive.mobile: Use a 4:3 or square screen ratio with controls wrapping below. Captions stay outside the media so they do not cover product details.
responsive.tablet: Maintain large screen area and place controls in a horizontal strip.
responsive.desktop: Use a large 16:9 or 5:3 screen with caption and status controls integrated below the bezel.

layout_hints.media_integrity: Reserve stable aspect ratio so demos do not shift layout when media loads.
layout_hints.evidence_first: The demo must reveal the product, not just vibe. Use realistic UI or product imagery.
layout_hints.controls: Use icon buttons where possible for play, pause, next, previous, and fullscreen. Cluster related mode controls together; place the primary conversion action at the far edge or in its own clear action area.

micro.control-press: button depresses with hard shadow collapse
micro.mode-switch: screen content swaps with quick pixel wipe under reduced motion fallback

transitions.screen-load: screen powers on with instant reveal and subtle LED blink.

keyboard: Media controls must be keyboard reachable.
Tabs or mode switches should use standard arrow-key behavior.
accessibility.focus_management: Fullscreen or modal demo views should trap focus and restore it to the launch control on close.
